lead: Material types, grade ladders, trader ratios, and storage caps on this page are verified against the sources below.

sources.label: Elite Dangerous Wiki — Materials
sources.what: The three types, grade-rarity bands, and that raw has no G5.

sources.label: Elite Dangerous Wiki — Material Trader
sources.what: Exchange ratios (6:1 up, 1:3 down, cross-group ×6), per-type separation, trader economies.

sources.label: Elite Dangerous Wiki — Raw Materials
sources.what: The 7 element groups, G1–G4 names, and surface/mining drop rules.

sources.label: Elite Dangerous Wiki — Manufactured & Encoded Materials
sources.what: The 10 manufactured chains and 6 encoded categories with their G1–G5 ladders.

sources.label: INARA — Components / Materials
sources.what: Trade-line counts (7 raw, 10 manufactured, 6 encoded = 23) and component grade bands.

sources.label: Elite Wiki (alioth.net) — Material Trader
sources.what: The full per-grade ratio matrix (up to 1,296:1) and the worked cross-grade math.

sources.label: EDSM — Stations (Material Trader service)
sources.what: Locating traders by station service and economy filters.

sources.label: ED Odyssey Materials Helper
sources.what: Desktop material tracker — inventory, wishlists, nearest-trader, ship-builder.

sources.label: ED Engineer
sources.what: Overlay tracker tying material stock to engineer blueprint requirements.

sources.label: ED Market Connector (EDCD)
sources.what: Companion uploader that syncs the journal to INARA and other web services.

sources.label: INARA — Commander materials
sources.what: Web inventory mirror and keep/sell guidance once an uploader is linked.

sources.label: YouTube — Mile 13 Gaming
sources.what: Tutorial on the fastest farming loop to max out high-grade Manufactured materials, updated for 2025.

sources.label: YouTube — Mile 13 Gaming
sources.what: Tutorial on farming Encoded materials at the Jameson crash site, updated for 2025.
